title: FIFA World Cup 2026: Raphinha goes off injured in Brazil vs Haiti
original_text: FIFA World Cup 2026: Raphinha goes off injured in Brazil vs Haiti left Brazil's Raphinha (11) leaves the pitch during the World Cup Group C match against Haiti. AP Photo/Petr David Josek https://ss-i.thgim.com/public/incoming/w8e2q5/article71124876.ece/alternates/FREE_1200/Haiti_Brazil_WCup_Soccer_499_8.jpg PETR DAVID JOSEK Brazil's Raphinha (11) leaves the pitch during the World Cup Group C soccer match between Brazil and Haiti in Philadelphia, Friday, June 19, 2026. (AP Photo/Petr David Josek) Raphinha experienced discomfort in the first half before being subbed off in the 40th minute for Rayan. CHENNAI Brazil suffered an injury blow when forward Raphinha went off injured in the FIFA World Cup 2026 match against Haiti at the Philadelphia Stadium on Friday. Raphinha experienced discomfort in the first half before being subbed off in the 40th minute for Rayan. Carlo Ancelotti will hope it’s not a serious injury for his No. 11 as he is a key starter for Brazil. “We will evaluate Raphinha tomorrow. Right now we’re not sure what happened,” said Ancelotti. The 29-year-old has had an injury-plagued season at Barcelona with recurring hamstring injuries. His teammate Vinicius Jr. said, “I think it’s the same one [injury] he had last time. I hope it’s not serious so he can stay with us.” He came into the World Cup with 15 goals and 5 assists for Barca, where it won the La Liga and Supercopa trophy.
